comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
[G3 child-coverage — container review of parent 1f19-adf2-ffb2-4b09 paired with this child (S5 Surface the ref + source controls on CLI + MCP schemas)]

Audited this child's contribution to the parent's acceptance/success criteria; every absence cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (S1 6d56, S2 70c7, S3 fa49, S4 30b3, S6 1ba5). This child is the client-surface slice (CLI flags + MCP tool-schema args + default wiring + surfacing verified_at_sha). The union of children covers every parent criterion. No uncovered parent criterion. Empty G3 findings list.

4-bucket coverage map (parent AC -> covering child/children; this child's role called out):
- Parent AC1 (no attested gate reads the working tree; all read a faithful snapshot at the pinned SHA) — FULLY covered by S1 (6d56 materialize core) + S3 (fa49 gate re-rooting). This child contributes the client-facing surface that decouples the verified path from server cwd/branch (its What: "The MCP server no longer depends on its own cwd/branch for the verified code path; document that REBAR_ROOT only locates the object DB to fetch from"). STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child — it exposes the controls but does not own the re-rooting (its Scope: "Out: the underlying behavior (S1-S4)"). Not an absence (S1/S3 cover it).
- Parent AC2 (signed verdict records verified_at_sha + reproducible over identical content) — FULLY covered by S4 (30b3 record+sign+in-toto) with the immutable-SHA basis from S1. This child is the PRIMARY OWNER of the SURFACING half: its AC2 (every code-reading MCP tool accepts ref+source; attested results surface verified_at_sha) + its AC3 (omitting --ref resolves origin/main->SHA in attested and still signs, pin recorded) make the recorded pin visible to clients. STRUCTURAL w.r.t. the signing itself (S4). Not an absence.
- Parent AC3 (local mode reads in-place checkout AND cannot produce a signed verdict) — covered by S3 (in-place read) + S4 (signing bar). This child is the PRIMARY OWNER of the SURFACING half: its AC4 (--source=local reachable from BOTH surfaces and reflected as unsigned in the result). Split is clean — S3/S4 enforce behavior, this child surfaces+reflects it; no uncovered half.
- Parent AC4 (concurrent gates, no git-lock contention / cross-op corruption; atomic population; delete-on-last-close) — FULLY covered by S1 (atomic population) + S2 (single-flight, delete-on-last-close). STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child; claims none. Not an absence.
- Parent AC5 (snapshot cache + janitor reclaim under pressure without disrupting in-flight reads) — FULLY covered by S2. STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child. Not an absence.
- Parent AC6 (private-repo fetch failure surfaces a descriptive, actionable credential error) — FULLY covered by S1 (raises; its AC5) + S6 (documents; its AC2). STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child. Not an absence.
- Parent AC7 (portable: no external tar; no hardcoded /tmp; flock+mkdir fallback) — FULLY covered by S1 (implementation; its AC4) + S6 (docs; its AC5). STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child. Not an absence.
- Parent AC8 (all six child stories closed; make lint + full test suite green) — STRUCTURAL/meta epic-completion criterion; covered by the existence of all six children (this child is story #5 of 6 in the roster) + each child's own green-bar obligation. Not an absence at this child.

Success-criteria narrative ("every code-reading gate verifies a CLIENT-SPECIFIED, immutable, reproducible snapshot ... never the server's mutable checkout ...; signed verdicts reproducible and bound to verified_at_sha; unsigned local preserves verify-before-push") decomposes onto AC1-AC3. The "CLIENT-SPECIFIED" clause is precisely this child's core deliverable: making ref+source reachable from BOTH the human (CLI) and agent (MCP) surfaces with documented origin/main defaults (its AC1/AC2/AC3) is what lets a distributed team target the exact code version a gate runs against. The "bound to verified_at_sha" clause is made client-visible here (surfaced in attested results); the "unsigned local" clause is reflected as unsigned in the result here. No uncovered obligation introduced.

Note: this child's "What" explicitly delegates underlying behavior to S1-S4 and prose docs to S6 (its Scope: "Out: the underlying behavior (S1-S4); prose docs (S6)"). Each delegated obligation is claimed by a named sibling — behavior by S1/S2/S3/S4, ref/source-semantics prose by S6 (its AC3 documents ref/source semantics + origin/main default for CLI and MCP). Covered-by-named-consumer, not absences.

Coverage-pairing note (clean for G3): this child (S5) and S3 (fa49) both list ref+source on the SAME five tool surfaces (review_plan/verify_completion/review_ticket/review_code/scan_spec) — S3 threads the parameters into the gate dispatch, this child exposes them on the CLI/MCP schema with defaults. This is a producer/surface split (parameter threading vs schema exposure), each scoped out of the other (S3 Scope: "Out: ... CLI/MCP surface schemas (S5)"; this child Scope: "Out: the underlying behavior (S1-S4)"). No double-ownership and no coverage gap — the client-surface obligation is claimed exactly once (here). (Any WHO-threads-it seam concern is a G4 matter, not a G3 union-coverage gap.)

Conclusion: this child cleanly covers the client-surface slice (CLI --ref/--source flags, MCP ref/source schema args, origin/main default wiring, surfacing verified_at_sha, reflecting local as unsigned) of the parent criteria — it is the primary owner of the "client-specified" clause and the surfacing half of parent AC2/AC3; no parent acceptance/success criterion is left uncovered by the roster. No G3 finding for this pairing.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
[G4 child-consistency — container review of parent 1f19-adf2-ffb2-4b09 paired with THIS child (S5 b7fa: Surface the ref + source controls on the CLI and MCP tool schemas)]

Ran the 7 cross-child interaction modes for this surface child against the WHOLE sibling roster (S1 6d56, S2 70c7, S3 fa49, S4 30b3, S6 1ba5). One high-confidence finding (scope overlap / dependency gap at the S3↔S5 code-review surface seam); the other 6 modes are clean under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter.

FINDING (scope overlap + dependency gap — the existing review_code base/head (+ --diff-file) surface is left unreconciled with the new single-`ref` control; neither S3 nor S5 owns retiring it on the SURFACE)
- finding: The parent collapses code-review to a SINGLE ref ("Diff-base (base+head) handling for code-review ... code-review gets a single repo copy at a single ref in this epic"). S3 (fa49) owns that change at the BEHAVIOR level (What: "Every operation takes ONE ref and gets ONE snapshot (code-review included; no base+head pair in this epic)"; AC3: "code-review uses a single snapshot at a single ref (no base+head pair) like the others"). THIS child (S5) owns the SURFACE and its What only says ADD `ref`+`source` to `review_code` (CLI `code-review` + MCP `review_code`) — it is SILENT on REMOVING/reconciling the pre-existing `base`/`head` (and CLI `--diff-file`) parameters those surfaces carry today. Result: as decomposed, S5 ADDS a single `--ref`/`ref` while the existing diff-range controls remain, leaving `review_code` advertising BOTH a single-ref snapshot control AND a base..head diff-range pair on the same surface — two contradictory code-selection controls on one tool, with no child claiming the retirement on the surface side.
- criteria: this child (S5) AC1 ("Every code-reading CLI command accepts --ref and --source with the documented defaults") + AC2 ("Every code-reading MCP tool accepts ref + source"); S3 (fa49) AC3 (single snapshot at a single ref, no base+head pair); parent Out-of-scope clause ("code-review gets a single repo copy at a single ref ... like every other operation").
- location: S5 (b7fa) What bullets 1-2 (CLI `code-review` + MCP `review_code` gain ref/source) + Scope ("Out: the underlying behavior (S1-S4)"); S3 (fa49) What ("code-review included; no base+head pair") + Scope ("Out: ... CLI/MCP surface schemas (S5)"); parent Out-of-scope (diff-base handling).
- evidence:
  1. Existing MCP `review_code` signature is a DIFF RANGE, not a single ref: src/rebar/mcp_server.py:595-599 — `def review_code(base: str = "HEAD~1", head: str = "HEAD", reviewers: list[str] | None = None)`. S5's "add ref+source to review_code" does not say what becomes of `base`/`head`.
  2. Existing CLI `rebar review-code` carries `--base`/`--head` AND `--diff-file`: src/rebar/_cli/_llm_commands.py:83-85 (`--base` default HEAD~1, `--head` default HEAD, `--diff-file`). S5's "add --ref/--source to ... code-review" does not say what becomes of `--base`/`--head`/`--diff-file`.
  3. S3 claims the single-ref BEHAVIOR collapse (its AC3) but explicitly scopes the surface OUT ("Out: ... CLI/MCP surface schemas (S5)") — so S3 does not retire the base/head SCHEMA/FLAGS.
  4. S5 claims the SURFACE but its What only ADDS ref/source and explicitly scopes the underlying behavior OUT ("Out: the underlying behavior (S1-S4)") — so S5 does not state the base/head retirement either.
  5. Roster-wide: no sibling's What/AC mentions removing/retiring/reconciling `base`/`head`/`--diff-file` on the code-review surface (search for "base+head"/"diff-file" finds only the existing CLI/tests/docs, no child obligation). The surface-side retirement is owned by neither S3 nor S5.
- scenarios: (a) Implementer ships S5 by ADDING `ref`/`source` to `review_code` while leaving `base`/`head`/`--diff-file` in place → the tool advertises a single-ref snapshot control alongside a base..head diff pair; an agent/human can pass `--ref X --base A --head B` and the schema gives no defined precedence — exactly the kind of ambiguous client-facing control the "client-specified ... exact code version" success-criterion is meant to make crisp. (b) Implementer reads parent + S3 and DROPS base/head on the surface, but since that retirement is in NEITHER child's stated scope it lands as undocumented surface-breakage (callers/tests at tests/interfaces/store/test_llm_optionality.py:126 use `review-code --diff-file`) with no child AC covering the migration. Either way S5 AC1/AC2 ("every code-reading command/tool accepts ref+source") is satisfiable while the code-review surface remains internally contradictory or silently broken.
- impact: The S3 (behavior: single ref) ↔ S5 (surface: schema/flags) seam leaves the base/head→single-ref RECONCILIATION ON THE SURFACE unowned. S5's AC1/AC2 only ADD ref/source; nothing in S5 (or S3, which scopes the surface out) retires/reconciles the pre-existing base/head/diff-file controls on `review_code`, so the parent's "single ref for code-review like every other operation" is not cleanly realized on the client surface this child owns.
- checklist_item: scope overlap / dependency gap (cross-child modes 4 + 3) at the S3↔S5 code-review surface seam
- suggested_fix: Make the surface-side retirement explicit and assign it. Preferred: extend THIS child (S5) with a What bullet + AC stating that `review_code`/`rebar review-code` REPLACE `base`/`head` (and resolve/retire `--diff-file`) with the single `--ref`/`ref` control (or define explicit precedence + deprecation), so the code-review surface exposes exactly one code-selection control consistent with S3's single-snapshot behavior. Cross-reference S3 AC3 so the behavior-and-surface halves of the base+head→single-ref collapse are jointly owned. (Symmetrically, S3 could name the surface retirement and hand it to S5 — but the obligation must live in one of the two by name.)

Clean on the other 6 modes:
- implicit shared state: the ref/source/verified_at_sha chain is a consistent producer→consumer flow — S1 resolves the immutable SHA, S3 threads it + re-roots, S4 records/signs verified_at_sha, THIS child surfaces ref/source + verified_at_sha on CLI/MCP, S6 documents the semantics + origin/main default. No surfaced value lacks a producer; defaults (origin/main) agree across S3/S5/S6.
- conflicting assumptions: attested-default-still-signs (this child AC3) and local-reflected-as-unsigned (this child AC4) agree with S3 (marks local unsigned, AC2), S4 (bars local signing, AC2), and S6 (documents attested-signs/local-never-signs). No contradiction.
- ordering violation: logical S1→S3→S4→this-child ordering; all six children carry empty deps uniformly and parent AC8 only requires all-closed + green, so no encoded ordering is violated.
- consumer impact: this child surfaces verified_at_sha (produced by S4) and exposes ref/source (threaded by S3) — it consumes those contracts without redefining them; it produces no new behavior a sibling must adapt to. The REBAR_ROOT-semantics doc bullet ("document that REBAR_ROOT only locates the object DB to fetch from") is this child's own What plus prose delegated to S6 (S6 AC3 ref/source semantics) — a benign schema-text-vs-prose split, not a broken downstream contract; README.md:193/:261 already carry REBAR_ROOT in the MCP-setup block.
- residual references: S1-S4 (Scope "Out: the underlying behavior (S1-S4)") and S6 (prose docs) all resolve to real roster siblings; the five named tool surfaces (review_plan/verify_completion/review_ticket/review_code/scan_spec) all exist in src/rebar/mcp_server.py. No dangling reference.

Note (NOT a G4 finding on THIS pairing): the standing dependency-gap finding on sibling S4 (30b3) about routing the pinned-SHA snapshot handle into plan_review/attest.py's dependency_hashes()/_rehash()/claim-gate freshness re-check is a code seam between S3 and S4; it does not touch this surface child and is out of this pairing's scope.

Conclusion: one high-confidence cross-child inconsistency stands — the base/head (+ --diff-file) code-review SURFACE retirement that pairs with S3's single-ref behavior collapse is owned by neither S3 nor this child (scope-overlap/dependency-gap at the S3↔S5 seam). The remaining 6 modes are clean.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
[G4 child-consistency — container review of parent 1f19-adf2-ffb2-4b09 paired with THIS child (S5 b7fa: Surface the ref + source controls on the CLI and MCP tool schemas)]

Ran the 7 cross-child interaction modes for this surface child against the WHOLE sibling roster (S1 6d56, S2 70c7, S3 fa49, S4 30b3, S6 1ba5). All 7 modes are clean under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter — EMPTY G4 findings list for this pairing.

Mode-by-mode:
- implicit shared state: The shared values this child surfaces all have a single named producer and a consistent producer→consumer chain. `ref`/`source` params: produced as a concept by the parent's "What", threaded into the gate dispatch by S3 (fa49), surfaced on CLI flags + MCP tool-schema args here. `verified_at_sha`: resolved by S1, threaded by S3, recorded/signed by S4 (30b3), surfaced in attested outputSchema/text here (this child AC2), documented by S6. `origin/main` default: this child AC1/AC3 ("--ref default origin/main"; "Omitting --ref resolves origin/main -> SHA") matches S3's default and S6's documented default. No shared-state divergence.
- conflicting assumptions: This child's surface claims agree with the behaviors siblings implement. AC3 ("Omitting --ref resolves origin/main -> SHA (attested) and still signs (pin recorded)") matches S4 AC1 (attested PASS records verified_at_sha + signs) and S1 (resolve ref -> immutable SHA). AC4 ("--source=local ... reflected as unsigned") matches S3 AC2 (local marks-unsigned) and S4 AC2 (local bars-signing). The five named tool surfaces (review_plan/verify_completion/review_ticket/review_code/scan_spec) are exactly S3's five gates. No contradiction.
- dependency gap: Every obligation this child surfaces is implemented by a named sibling — param threading by S3 (its file_impact: completion.py/pai_tools.py/mcp_server.py), signing/verified_at_sha by S4, SHA resolution by S1, prose semantics by S6. This child's own scope ("Out: the underlying behavior (S1-S4); prose docs (S6)") delegates each piece to a present sibling. The standing roster dependency-gap (the pinned-SHA snapshot handle not routed into plan_review/attest.py's dependency_hashes()/_rehash()/freshness re-check) is a seam between S3 and S4; it is downstream of this surface child and does not touch the CLI/MCP schema work. No gap originated or left unowned at THIS child.
- scope overlap: Two potential overlaps, both clean. (a) ref/source documentation: this child surfaces "documented defaults" inline in the schema (machine-facing outputSchema/text), while S6 owns the human PROSE (S6 AC3); S5 explicitly scopes "prose docs (S6)" out and S6 scopes "code (S1-S5)" out — a schema-text vs prose split, not double-ownership of one artifact (consistent with the S6 G4 review already on record). (b) S5 What bullet 3 ("the MCP server no longer depends on its own cwd/branch ...; document that REBAR_ROOT only locates the object DB") restates the re-rooting outcome S3 owns (S3 AC5: "repo_root only locates the object DB to fetch from; the verified code is the snapshot") — but S5 only SURFACES/documents that fact at the schema layer (its "What" frames it as the no-cwd-dependence note), it does not re-implement the re-rooting; S3 owns the behavior. Producer/surface split, no double-implementation.
- ordering violation: Logical S1->S3->S4->this-child ordering with S6 documenting; all six children carry empty deps uniformly and parent AC8 only requires all-closed + green, so no encoded ordering is violated (consistent with the roster-wide posture in prior reviews).
- consumer impact: This child is a terminal surface/consumer — it consumes S3's threaded ref/source params and S4's verified_at_sha to expose them on CLI/MCP; it produces no new behavior contract that a sibling must satisfy, so it imposes no downstream breakage.
- residual references: review_plan/verify_completion/review_ticket/review_code/scan_spec (S3's five gates), verified_at_sha (S4), the S1-S4 underlying-behavior scope-out and S6 prose-docs scope-out, REBAR_ROOT (real env in config.py:26 / README.md:193,261), origin/main default — all resolve to real producers/siblings/symbols. No dangling reference.

Note (not a G4 finding on THIS pairing): the standing dependency-gap finding on sibling S4 (30b3) about routing the pinned-SHA snapshot handle into plan_review/attest.py (dependency_hashes()/_rehash()/the ADR-0002 freshness re-check, all rooted on _config.repo_root) is a code seam between S3 and S4; it is unrelated to this CLI/MCP-surface child and out of this pairing's scope. Likewise the S1/S3 conflicting-assumptions seam (whether materialize() runs in local mode) flagged on S1 does not touch this surface child.

Conclusion: no high-confidence cross-child inconsistency between this surface child (S5) and the parent/siblings — the ref/source CLI+MCP surfacing and verified_at_sha exposure are a clean producer/surface split over S3's threading and S4's signing, with prose docs cleanly delegated to S6. Empty G4 findings list for this pairing.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: PLAN-REVIEW TRIAGE (verdict PASS; 8 advisories). Accepted: (1) resolve the CLI<->MCP naming drift and ENUMERATE the exact commands/tools that gain the flags (MCP: review_plan/verify_completion/review_ticket/review_code/scan_spec; pin the CLI counterparts rather than 'surfaces as they exist'); (2) add a FAILURE-PATH AC — unresolvable/absent ref, invalid --source value, object DB unreachable at REBAR_ROOT — so the controls are testable by construction; (3) add an AC for the cwd/branch decoupling + REBAR_ROOT-only-locates-object-DB behavior; (4) consider landing a thin vertical slice (one CLI cmd + one MCP tool end-to-end) before fanning out to all surfaces. None invalid.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
[G3 child-coverage — container review of parent 1f19-adf2-ffb2-4b09 paired with THIS child (S5 b7fa: Surface the ref + source controls on the CLI and MCP tool schemas)]

Audited this child's contribution to the parent's acceptance/success criteria; every absence cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (S1 6d56 materialize core, S2 70c7 cache+janitor, S3 fa49 gate threading, S4 30b3 attested signing, S6 1ba5 docs). This child is the CLI/MCP surfacing slice; the union of children covers every parent criterion. No uncovered parent criterion. EMPTY G3 findings list.

4-bucket coverage map (parent AC -> covering child/children; this child's role called out):
- Parent AC1 (no attested gate reads the working tree; all read a faithful snapshot materialized at the pinned SHA) — FULLY covered by S1 (materialize core) + S3 (gate re-rooting onto the snapshot). This child SURFACES the ref/source controls that let a client target the snapshot, and its AC5 verifies the cwd/branch decoupling (the verified-code path does not depend on the MCP server's checked-out branch; REBAR_ROOT only locates the object DB). Surface contribution; the snapshot-only read behavior is owned by S1/S3 (this child's Scope: "Out: the underlying behavior (S1-S4)"). Not an absence.
- Parent AC2 (signed verdict records verified_at_sha + reproducible over identical content) — FULLY covered by S4 (record+sign+in-toto) atop S1's immutable-SHA basis. This child is the SURFACE PRIMARY OWNER of the verified_at_sha exposure: its AC3 (attested results surface verified_at_sha) + its AC2 (omitting --ref resolves origin/main -> SHA in attested and STILL signs, pin recorded). Surface half; signing behavior owned by S4. Not an absence.
- Parent AC3 (local mode reads in-place checkout AND cannot produce a signed verdict) — covered by S3 (in-place read) + S4 (signing bar). This child makes --source=local REACHABLE from BOTH surfaces and reflects it as unsigned in the result (its AC2). Surface contribution; the reader/enforcer behavior is S3/S4. Not an absence.
- Parent AC4 (concurrent gates on same/different SHAs run without git-lock contention or cross-op corruption; no worktrees; atomic population; delete-on-last-close) — FULLY covered by S1 (atomic population, no-worktree) + S2 (single-flight, delete-on-last-close). Explicitly out of this child's scope (its Scope: "Out: the underlying behavior (S1-S4)"); claims none. STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child. Not an absence.
- Parent AC5 (snapshot cache + janitor reclaim disk under pressure without disrupting in-flight reads) — FULLY covered by S2 (sole owner). STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child (out of its scope). Not an absence.
- Parent AC6 (private-repo fetch failure surfaces a descriptive, actionable credential error) — the credential-error facet is FULLY covered by S1 (raises; its AC5) + S6 (documents message+remedy; its AC1/AC2). This child owns the COMPLEMENTARY surface-level validation/failure paths: its AC4 (an unresolvable/absent ref, an invalid --source value, and an unreachable object DB at REBAR_ROOT each return a clear, actionable error — attested fails closed — covered by tests; explicitly cross-refs "cf. S1 descriptive fetch/auth errors"). The fetch/credential error itself originates in S1's coalesced fetch; this child's AC4 hardens the surface around it. Not an absence (credential error = S1+S6; surface-validation = this child).
- Parent AC7 (portable: no external tar; no hardcoded /tmp; flock+mkdir fallback) — FULLY covered by S1 (implementation; its AC4) + S6 (docs: REBAR_GATE_TMPDIR + EFS/NFS flock caveat; its AC5). STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child. Not an absence.
- Parent AC8 (all six child stories closed; make lint + full test suite green) — STRUCTURAL/meta epic-completion criterion; covered by the existence of all six children (this child is story #5 of 6 in the roster) + each child's own green-bar obligation. Not an absence at this child.

Success-criteria narrative ("every code-reading gate verifies a CLIENT-SPECIFIED, immutable, reproducible snapshot of the project at a pinned SHA — never the server's mutable checkout — safely under concurrent use ...; signed verdicts reproducible and bound to verified_at_sha; the unsigned local mode preserves the single-developer verify-before-push workflow") decomposes onto AC1-AC3. The "CLIENT-SPECIFIED" clause is precisely this child's core deliverable: surfacing --ref/ref + --source/source on BOTH the CLI and the MCP tool schemas (the five operations: review_plan/verify_completion/review_ticket/review_code/scan_spec) with a pinned one-to-one CLI<->MCP mapping is what makes the snapshot client-targetable rather than implicit. Its AC1 (every code-reading CLI command AND its MCP counterpart accept the two controls with documented defaults; the mapping covers the same five operations one-to-one, no naming-drift coverage gap) and AC6 (a thin vertical slice lands before the full fan-out) deliver that surface. "verified_at_sha" exposure is this child's AC3; "unsigned local" reachability/reflection is its AC2. No uncovered obligation introduced.

Named-consumer / delegation cross-checks (covered-by-named-consumer, not absences):
- This child's Scope explicitly delegates the underlying behavior to named siblings — "Out: the underlying behavior (S1-S4)" and "Out: ... prose docs (S6)". Each delegated obligation is claimed by the named sibling (materialization S1 6d56; cache/janitor S2 70c7; gate threading S3 fa49; signing S4 30b3; prose ref/source semantics S6 1ba5 AC3). Covered-by-named-consumer.
- This child's "Document defaults + surface verified_at_sha (outputSchema/text)" is the machine-facing schema-text surface; the human PROSE ref/source semantics it scopes out are S6's AC3 (clean schema-text-vs-prose split; each claims its artifact once).

Coverage-pairing note (clean for G3): this child (S5) and S3 (fa49) both name ref+source on the SAME five operations (review_plan/verify_completion/review_ticket/review_code/scan_spec). S3 THREADS the parameters into the gate dispatch and re-roots the file tools onto the snapshot; S5 EXPOSES them on the CLI/MCP schema with documented defaults and the pinned one-to-one CLI<->MCP mapping (resolving review/review_plan and code-review/review_code naming so coverage matches one-to-one). Producer/surface split, each scoped out of the other (S5: "Out: the underlying behavior (S1-S4)"; S3: "Out: ... CLI/MCP surface schemas (S5)"). No double-ownership, no coverage gap; the one-to-one mapping AC (this child's AC1) closes the naming-drift risk between the two surfaces.

Note (scope, NOT a G3 finding): the standing G4 dependency-gap on sibling S4 (30b3) — routing the pinned-SHA snapshot handle into plan_review/attest.py's dependency_hashes()/_rehash()/claim-gate freshness re-check across the S3<->S4 seam — is a CONSISTENCY (G4) concern about WHO threads the handle, not a G3 union-coverage gap, and is unrelated to this surfacing child. The corresponding parent obligations (AC1 snapshot-only reads, AC2 reproducibility, ref-coherence) remain CLAIMED by children, so the union covers them. G3 union-coverage of the parent criteria is complete.

Conclusion: this child cleanly covers the CLI/MCP surfacing slice (--ref/ref + --source/source on all five CLI commands AND their MCP counterparts with documented defaults and a one-to-one mapping; origin/main default still signs; local reachable+reflected-unsigned from both surfaces; verified_at_sha surfaced; surface-level validation/failure paths fail closed; cwd/branch decoupling proven; vertical-slice-first sequencing) of the parent criteria — it is the surface primary owner of the "client-specified" success-criteria clause and the verified_at_sha exposure half of AC2. No parent acceptance/success criterion is left uncovered by the roster. No G3 finding for this pairing.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
[G3 child-coverage — container review of parent 1f19-adf2-ffb2-4b09 paired with THIS child (S5 b7fa: Surface the ref + source controls on the CLI and MCP tool schemas)]

4-bucket audit of this child's contribution to the parent's acceptance/success criteria; every absence cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (S1 6d56 materialize core, S2 70c7 cache+janitor, S3 fa49 gate threading, S4 30b3 attested signing, S6 1ba5 docs). The union of children covers every parent criterion; no uncovered parent criterion. EMPTY G3 findings list.

Coverage map (parent AC -> covering child; this child's role):
- AC1 (no attested gate reads working tree; all read faithful snapshot at pinned SHA) — FULLY by S1+S3. This child SURFACES ref/source + its AC5 proves cwd/branch decoupling (REBAR_ROOT only locates object DB). Surface contribution; behavior scoped out ("Out: the underlying behavior (S1-S4)"). Not an absence.
- AC2 (signed verdict records verified_at_sha + reproducible) — FULLY by S4 atop S1. This child is SURFACE owner of verified_at_sha exposure (its AC3) + origin/main resolves & still signs (its AC2). Not an absence.
- AC3 (local reads in-place + cannot sign) — by S3 (read) + S4 (signing bar). This child makes --source=local reachable from BOTH surfaces and reflects unsigned (its AC2). Not an absence.
- AC4 (concurrent gates, no lock contention/corruption; atomic population; delete-on-last-close) — FULLY by S1+S2. STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child (scoped out). Not an absence.
- AC5 (cache+janitor reclaim under pressure without disrupting reads) — FULLY by S2 (sole owner). STRUCTURAL. Not an absence.
- AC6 (private-repo fetch failure -> descriptive credential error) — credential facet by S1 (raises) + S6 (documents). This child owns COMPLEMENTARY surface validation (its AC4: unresolvable/absent ref, invalid --source, unreachable object DB each fail closed with actionable error, cf. S1). Not an absence.
- AC7 (portable: no external tar; no hardcoded /tmp; flock+mkdir fallback) — FULLY by S1 (impl) + S6 (docs). STRUCTURAL. Not an absence.
- AC8 (all six stories closed; lint + tests green) — STRUCTURAL/meta epic-completion; covered by existence of all six children (this is #5 of 6) + each child's green-bar obligation. Not an absence.

Success-criteria narrative decomposes onto AC1-AC3; the "CLIENT-SPECIFIED" clause is precisely this child's core deliverable (ref/source reachable on both CLI and MCP across the five operations with one-to-one mapping). verified_at_sha exposure = its AC3; unsigned-local reflection = its AC2; client-targetable snapshot = its AC1/AC6 (vertical slice). No uncovered obligation introduced.

Delegation cross-checks (covered-by-named-consumer, not absences): "Out: the underlying behavior (S1-S4)" -> S1/S2/S3/S4; "Out: prose docs (S6)" -> S6 AC3 (schema-text-vs-prose split, each artifact claimed once).

Coverage-pairing note (clean): S5 and S3 both name ref+source on the same five operations — producer/surface split (S3 threads into dispatch; S5 exposes on schema), each scoped out of the other, no double-ownership and no gap; the one-to-one mapping AC (this child's AC1) closes naming-drift. (WHO-threads-it seam is a G4 matter, not a G3 union-coverage gap.)

Conclusion: this child cleanly covers the CLI/MCP surfacing slice of the parent criteria; no parent acceptance/success criterion is left uncovered by the roster. No G3 finding for this pairing.

comments.body: FORCE_CLAIM: plan-review gate bypassed by user approval — no plan-review attestation was verified. Reason: "Plan PASS, stale only by HEAD movement; implementing S5 surfaces (S3 dep closed).".

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: Added --ref/--source to all 5 CLI commands (_llm_commands.py: _review/_review_code/_scan_spec/_verify_completion/_review_plan via shared _add_ref_source) AND the 5 MCP tools (mcp_server.py: review_ticket/review_code/scan_spec/verify_completion/review_plan), one-to-one. AC1 pinned CLI<->MCP mapping (no naming drift), proven by an MCP list_tools inputSchema introspection test + per-command CLI threading test; AC2 defaults None->config-resolved (origin/main+attested; review-code ref defaults to the reviewed head), local reachable on both surfaces + reflected unsigned; AC3 verified_at_sha surfaced (JSON passes it through; text via _render_source_line); AC4 invalid --source -> argparse exit 2, unresolvable/absent ref + unreachable object DB -> SnapshotError caught -> clean Error + exit 1 (attested fails closed); AC5 cwd/branch decoupling covered by the S3 regression (REBAR_ROOT only locates the object DB); AC6 vertical slice (review-ticket end-to-end) validated first. Subagent review: NO blocking issues, all 6 ACs met; folded its finding (review-code --ref help no longer overstates REBAR_GATE_REF configurability). 6 S5 tests + 569 mcp/cli/llm tests green (1 pre-existing @integration jira test unrelated); ruff clean.

compiled_state.description:
# Surface the ref + source controls on the CLI and MCP tool schemas

## Why

The new `ref` + `source` controls must be reachable by both human (CLI) and agent (MCP)
clients, so a distributed team can target the exact code version a gate runs against.

## What

- Add `--ref <branch|tag|sha>` (default `origin/main`) and `--source {attested|local}` to the
  code-reading surfaces, with the CLI<->MCP mapping pinned explicitly (no "surfaces as they
  exist"):
  - MCP tools: `review_plan`, `verify_completion`, `review_ticket`, `review_code`, `scan_spec`.
  - CLI counterparts (the same five operations as exposed by the CLI); enumerate the exact
    command names in the implementation and keep the two surfaces in lockstep (resolve the
    review/review_plan and code-review/review_code naming so coverage matches one-to-one).
- Document defaults + surface `verified_at_sha` in attested results (outputSchema/text).
- Decouple the verified-code path from the MCP server's cwd/branch; document that `REBAR_ROOT`
  only locates the object DB to fetch from.
- **Validation / failure paths** (not just happy path): an unresolvable or absent `ref`, an
  invalid `--source` value, and an unreachable/missing object DB at `REBAR_ROOT` each produce
  a clear, actionable error (attested fails closed; cf. S1 descriptive fetch/auth errors).
- **De-risk by vertical slice:** land ONE CLI command + ONE MCP tool end-to-end first
  (default `origin/main` resolve + attested `verified_at_sha` + `local`=unsigned) before
  fanning the two controls out across all five surfaces.

## Scope

- In: CLI flags, MCP tool-schema args, the pinned CLI<->MCP mapping, default wiring, failure/
  validation handling, surfacing `verified_at_sha`, the vertical-slice-first sequencing.
- Out: the underlying behavior (S1-S4); prose docs (S6).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Every code-reading CLI command AND its MCP tool counterpart accept `--ref`/`ref` and
      `--source`/`source` with documented defaults; the CLI<->MCP mapping covers the same five
      operations one-to-one (no naming-drift coverage gap).
- [ ] Omitting `--ref` resolves `origin/main` -> SHA (attested) and still signs (pin recorded);
      `--source=local` is reachable from both surfaces and reflected as unsigned in the result.
- [ ] Attested results surface `verified_at_sha`.
- [ ] An unresolvable/absent ref, an invalid `--source` value, and an unreachable object DB at
      `REBAR_ROOT` each return a clear actionable error (attested fails closed) — covered by tests.
- [ ] An AC verifies the cwd/branch decoupling: the verified-code path does not depend on the
      MCP server's checked-out branch; `REBAR_ROOT` only locates the object DB.
- [ ] A thin vertical slice (one CLI cmd + one MCP tool, end-to-end) lands before the full fan-out.

## Additional notes + Acceptance Criteria (advisory remediation)

- Pinned CLI<->MCP names (mirror the MCP tool names, confirm against the command registry):
  `review-plan`<->`review_plan`, `verify-completion`<->`verify_completion`,
  `review-ticket`<->`review_ticket`, `review-code`<->`review_code`, `scan-spec`<->`scan_spec`.
- `--source` default is `attested` (stated explicitly); `--ref` default is `origin/main`.
- The cross-surface contract (one-to-one CLI<->MCP mapping) and the cwd/branch decoupling
  (`REBAR_ROOT` only locates the object DB) are captured in the architecture ADR (S2/S4), so
  future maintainers don't re-couple the verified-code path to the server's checkout.
- Error recovery: each actionable error names the remedy target — an unresolvable/absent ref
  -> how to name a valid ref; an invalid `--source` -> the allowed enum; an unreachable
  `REBAR_ROOT`/object DB or missing credentials -> the setup-docs section (S6).

- [ ] The five CLI commands and five MCP tools are the pinned one-to-one set above (no
      naming-drift gap); `--source` defaults to `attested`, `--ref` to `origin/main`.
- [ ] Each failure-path error names its recovery target (valid-ref guidance / allowed --source
      enum / REBAR_ROOT+credentials docs), consistent across both surfaces.
- [ ] The cwd/branch decoupling + CLI<->MCP mapping are recorded in the architecture ADR.
compiled_state.title: Surface the ref + source controls on the CLI and MCP tool schemas
